subject: [PATCH v2 0/4] memcg: shrink obj_stock_pcp and cache multiple objcgs

reviews.author: Qi Zheng
reviews.summary: Reviewer Qi Zheng noted that the patch should change the condition for refilling obj_stock_pcp to avoid overflowing a uint16_t, and acknowledged the patch with an Acked-by tag.
reviews.raw_body:
^

should also be changed to: don't refill > U16_MAX ?

Otherwise:

Acked-by: Qi Zheng <qi.zheng@linux.dev>

Thanks!

reviews.author: Muchun Song
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Muchun Song requested that the patch author fix the comment numbering sequence, noting that it was incorrect and had been present for some time.

Reviewer Muchun Song requested the patch author to provide a detailed explanation of why the multi-slot pattern is necessary, specifically asking for an analysis of its performance benefits and whether it can be reverted if needed.
reviews.raw_body:
^
           4

Since you're already modifying the comments in this section,
would you mind fixing the numbering as well? I noticed that the
sequence was wrong a while back :)

---

^
           5

Thanks,
Muchun

reviews.author: David Laight
reviews.summary:
Reviewer David Laight noted that the patch does not handle the case where the obj_stock_pcp is already locked, which could lead to a deadlock when trying to acquire the lock again in reclaim paths.

Reviewer David Laight suggested reordering the code to first add bytes to the cache and then check if it can be stored in a single slot, otherwise calling mod_objcg_mlstate() to update the object's memory layout state.

reviews.author: Shakeel Butt (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged the need for a fix and promised to address it in a future patch or by asking another developer to make the change.
sentiment_signals: acknowledged the need for a fix
reviews.raw_body:
Thanks. If I send a new version, I will fix this otherwise I will ask Andrew to
fix this inplace.

reviews.author: Shakeel Butt (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledges that the patch needs further revision and promises to either fix it in v2 or request someone else (Andrew) to make the necessary changes.
reviews.raw_body:
Haha I didn't even notice. If I send a new version, I will fix this otherwise I
will ask Andrew to fix inplace.

reviews.author: Andrew Morton
reviews.summary: Reviewer asked about a pending review request from Sashiko, inquiring whether the patch author had seen it and its associated email notification.
reviews.raw_body:
Thanks, I added this to mm.git's mm-new branch, along with a couple of
possible todo notes from the review.

Sashiko asked a thing:
	https://sashiko.dev/#/patchset/20260522011908.1669332-1-shakeel.butt@linux.dev

Did you already see this?  The footers there indicate that an email was
sent out but I don't know if it works?

2026-05-22.patch_summary: This patch series addresses a performance regression caused by the introduction of per-node obj_cgroup in commit 01b9da291c49. The regression occurs when threads of the same memcg run on different NUMA nodes, causing the per-CPU obj_stock_pcp to thrash. To fix this, the patches shrink the existing single-slot obj_stock_pcp fields and convert cached_objcg and nr_bytes into slot arrays using a multi-slot pattern already used by memcg_stock_pcp. This approach allows multiple objcgs per CPU to coexist in the stock without forcing a drain, making it more efficient than the temporary fix introduced earlier.

reviews.author: Shakeel Butt (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a specific concern about archs with 256KiB base page sizes and promised to fix it in v3.
reviews.raw_body:
Yes, I saw that comment. It is kind of very specific to archs with 256KiB base
page sizes. Anyways, I have a simple fix for that and there were minor
suggestions from others for simple changes, I will send v3 with the requested
changes.

2026-05-25.patch_summary: This patch series addresses a performance regression caused by the introduction of per-node obj_cgroup in commit 01b9da291c49. The regression occurs when threads of the same memcg run on different NUMA nodes, causing the per-CPU obj_stock_pcp to thrash. To fix this, the patches shrink the existing single-slot obj_stock_pcp fields and convert cached_objcg and nr_bytes into slot arrays using a multi-slot pattern already used by memcg_stock_pcp. This approach allows multiple objcgs per CPU to coexist in the stock without forcing a drain, making it more efficient than the temporary fix introduced earlier.
